pattern: Every distinct board is found by row-by-row backtracking, rendering each accepted column choice into a string row of dots with a single queen.
hints: Place one queen per row and only choose a column that no earlier queen attacks.
Columns and both diagonal directions (row-col and row+col) capture every attack as set membership.
Backtrack across rows recording each column; when all rows are filled, render every row as a string of dots with 'Q' at the chosen column and collect that board.

edge_inputs.description: smallest board with two distinct solutions
single cell has one trivial board
three by three has no solution
five by five has ten solutions

stress.description: deterministically sample a small legal board size
stress.note: n capped at 7 keeps the rendered solution set small while exercising many boards
